If you can speak but do not hear AI responses, see Lip-Sync, or subtitles displaying text above Mike, fully exit the game and restart it. In some cases, restarting the application can restore audio behavior.

This can help in both: - the desktop application version through Meta Quest Link - the APK version

docs.title: 4. If problems continue
docs.text:
If the interview still does not work as expected after checking microphone mute status and, when applicable, app permissions:

  • close the game completely and reopen it,
  • confirm the headset microphone is unmuted,
  • if using the APK version, confirm the app still has microphone permission enabled,
  • then try the interview again.

If the issue still persists after that, additional setup or device-specific troubleshooting may be required.

docs.title: Launching the Game
docs.text: This guide shows you how to find and open DeVILSona on your Meta Quest headset.

docs.title: How to Launch DeVILSona
docs.text:
1. Put on the headset
  2. Press the Oculus button on the right-hand controller to open the universal menu

  3. Open the App Library (the grid icon at the bottom of the home screen)

  4. Click on the Menu button and navigate to the "Unknown Sources" tab — since DeVILSona was installed manually, it appears here instead of the main app list

  5. Look for "FSE100Capstone" (or similar) in the list

  6. Tap it to launch the game — you should see the login screen

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Can't find the app? Make sure you're looking under the "Unknown Sources" tab, not the main app list. DeVILSona won't appear in the regular library because it was sideloaded.
  • App crashes or shows a black screen? The game files may not have been fully installed. Ask your instructor or lab staff to reinstall the app on the headset.
  • Login screen doesn't appear? Try closing and reopening the app. If the issue continues, let your instructor know.

docs.title: Troubleshooting Navigation Issues
docs.text: This page covers common navigation, positioning, and orientation issues that may occur while using the VR experience.

docs.title: Common Navigation Issues
docs.text:
Users may occasionally experience one or more of the following:

  • feeling disoriented after teleporting in a scenario that uses teleport movement
  • facing the wrong direction after a scene loads
  • being positioned too far away from an object they need to interact with
  • feeling slightly offset from where they are expected to stand or sit
  • confusion about whether a scenario uses teleport movement at all

docs.title: Resetting Your View
docs.text: If you appear in the wrong location or are facing the wrong direction, try resetting your view. The correct method depends on whether you are using the desktop application through Meta Quest Link or the APK version installed directly on the headset.

docs.title: Desktop Application Version (Meta Quest Link)
docs.text:
When playing the desktop application through Meta Quest Link, you can reset your orientation using either the Meta Quest Link menu or, if available in the current scenario, the in-game reset option.

Option 1: Meta Quest Link Reset View 1. Press the Meta button on the right controller. 2. Select Reset View.

This is typically the most direct way to re-center yourself while using the desktop application version.

Option 2: In-Game Reset Orientation Some parts of the project also provide a Reset Orientation option through the in-game menu. This may be accessible through:

  • the watch widget (Y button on left controller or B button on right controller)
  • the Menu button with the three horizontal lines on the left controller

If this option is available and functional in the current scenario, it can also be used while playing the desktop application version.

docs.title: APK Version
docs.text:
When playing the APK version installed directly on the headset, pressing the Meta button does not provide a Reset View option for the scenario. Instead, it typically only provides the option to quit the application.

For the APK version, you must rely on the in-game reset option instead.

In-Game Reset Orientation If available in the current scenario, use Reset Orientation through:

  • the watch widget (Y button on left controller or B button on right controller)
  • the Menu button with the three horizontal lines on the left controller

For the APK version, this is the primary method for resetting your orientation while remaining in the app.

docs.title: Teleportation and Disorientation
docs.text:
Some scenarios use teleport movement. If you are unfamiliar with VR teleport controls, it is normal to feel briefly disoriented after teleporting to a new position.

To reduce disorientation:

  • pause briefly after teleporting before continuing
  • re-check which direction you are facing before moving again
  • use the appropriate reset method if you no longer feel aligned correctly
  • avoid repeated rapid teleporting if you begin to feel uncomfortable

docs.title: Seated vs. Standing Play
docs.text:
For the best experience, choose either seated or standing play before starting a scenario and remain consistent throughout that session when possible.

Switching between sitting and standing during a scenario can sometimes affect your alignment, eye level, or distance from interactable objects. If this happens, use the appropriate reset option to re-center yourself.

docs.title: Scenario-Specific Movement Differences
docs.text:
Not all scenarios use navigation in the same way.

  • Some scenarios require the player to teleport manually between areas.
  • Some scenarios place the player automatically where they need to be.
  • Some scenarios may support menu-based reset tools, while others may not.

If movement feels incorrect, first confirm whether the scenario is supposed to use teleportation or automatic positioning.

docs.title: If You Feel Out of Place in the Environment
docs.text:
If you are too close to an object, too far away, or facing the wrong direction:

  1. Stop moving for a moment.
  2. Check whether the scenario expects you to teleport or remain where you are.
  3. Use the appropriate reset method for your platform:
  4. Desktop app through Meta Quest Link: press the Meta button and select Reset View, or use Reset Orientation through the watch widget or Menu button if available
  5. APK version: use Reset Orientation through the watch widget or Menu button
  6. Reposition your real body slightly if needed.
  7. If the issue continues, exit and restart the scenario.

docs.title: General VR Comfort Tips
docs.text:
Navigation problems can sometimes feel worse when combined with general VR discomfort. To reduce this:

  • take a short break if you begin to feel dizzy, disoriented, or uncomfortable
  • avoid rushing through repeated movement actions
  • make sure your play area is clear and safe before continuing
  • stop the experience if discomfort becomes significant

docs.title: When to Restart the Experience
docs.text:
Restarting the current scenario may help if:

  • your position appears clearly incorrect after loading
  • reset tools do not fix the issue
  • your facing direction remains wrong
  • you cannot reach an interaction point that should be within range

If restarting does not help, additional scenario-specific troubleshooting may be needed.

docs.title: Troubleshooting Scenario 1: Morning Routine
docs.text: This guide covers common issues you might encounter while playing Scenario 1: Morning Routine and provides steps to resolve them.

docs.title: 1. Right Hand is Invisible or Missing
docs.text:
Issue: When the scenario starts, you cannot see your right hand or use it to interact with objects.

Solution: - This is intentional. In this scenario, the protagonist is missing their right arm. Your right hand will remain invisible until you complete the objective to equip the prosthetic arm. - However, a guide ray should still be visible when you point at UI menus. - To resolve this, simply progress through the scenario: proceed to the shower room and equip the prosthetic arm using your left hand. Once equipped, your right hand will become fully visible and functional.

docs.title: 2. Difficulty Adjusting the Shower Temperature
docs.text:
Issue: You are unable to turn on the shower or adjust the temperature dial to fill the completion gauge.

Solution: - Grabbing the Dial: Ensure you are using your left hand and that you are close enough to the showerhead. Press and hold the interact button (Trigger/Grip) to grab the dial. - Turning the Dial: While holding the dial, physically rotate your controller. Turn to the left for hot water and to the right for cold water. - Gauge Not Filling: If the gauge stops filling, it means the water temperature is no longer optimal. Keep adjusting the dial slightly left or right until the gauge starts filling up again. - Turning it Off: Remember to press the button on the showerhead again to stop the water once the gauge is full.

docs.title: 3. Prosthetic Arm Won't Attach
docs.text:
Issue: You have reached the prosthetic arm but it won't equip when you try to grab it.

Solution: - You must use your left hand to grab the prosthetic arm. - Make sure you are standing close enough to the arm hanging in the shower room. Aim at it until the interaction highlight appears, then press the interact button. - Save System Synchronization: If you have completed all previous conditions and the arm still won't attach, there might be a temporary issue with the AWS save system. Please exit to the main menu and log back in. Upon returning, you should be able to equip the arm normally.

docs.title: 4. Issues Interacting with Mary (AI Character)
docs.text:
Issue: Mary is not responding to your voice, or the virtual keyboard is not appearing.

Solution: - Microphone Issues: Check if your microphone is muted or if "Speech to Text" is disabled in the Quick Menu settings. - OpenAI Connection: If the server connection drops, the game will automatically switch to offline mode. In this case, use the [Choose Questions] menu to proceed with scripted dialogue. - For more detailed AI interaction troubleshooting, please refer to the Troubleshooting Interview Audio and Microphone Issues guide.

docs.title: 5. Cannot Drink Coffee / Scenario Won't Complete
docs.text:
Issue: You grabbed the coffee cup, but the scenario completion screen does not trigger.

Solution: - Make sure you have completed all previous objectives in order (Wake up → Shower → Equip Prosthetic Arm → Talk to Mary/Pet Dog). The coffee objective is the final step. - Ensure you are physically bringing the coffee cup close to your headset (mouth area) to trigger the drinking action.

If you are experiencing general movement or VR sickness issues, please check the Troubleshooting Navigation Issues guide.

docs.title: Scenario 1: Morning Routine
docs.text:
When the game starts, your objectives will be displayed on the screen. You will need to complete the following steps in order: Wake up, Take a shower, Equip your prosthetic arm, and Drink coffee.

Note: Your right hand will remain invisible until you equip the prosthetic arm. However, for your convenience, a guide ray will still be visible when operating the UI.

docs.title: 1. Waking Up
docs.text:
• This is the most basic step designed to help you practice using the joystick.
  • Mission: Simply move to the designated location.
  • Movement: You will use Teleportation to navigate. Note that you can teleport through obstacles like glass doors.

docs.title: 2. Taking a Shower
docs.text:
• Move to the shower room. Place your left hand on the showerhead and press the interact button to turn on the water.
  • Water Temperature: The key to this task is adjusting the water temperature. Turn the dial to the left for hot water and to the right for cold water.
  • Completion: Successfully adjusting the temperature will fill up the gauge. Once the gauge is completely full, the shower task is complete. Press the button on the showerhead one more time to stop the water.

docs.title: 3. Equipping the Prosthetic Arm
docs.text: * Approach the prosthetic arm hanging inside the shower room. * Grab it with your left hand. Once equipped, your right hand will visibly appear as the prosthetic arm.

docs.title: 4. Interacting in the Living Room
docs.text:
Mary (Wife NPC): Move to the living room where you will find Mary, Mike's wife. She is highly supportive of him. Approach her to initiate an interview interaction.

Pet Dog: You can pet the dog on the head to trigger a simple, friendly interaction.

docs.title: 5. Completing the Scenario
docs.text:
• Finally, grab the coffee resting on the table.

Doing this completes the Morning Routine scenario. A menu UI will then appear, prompting you to either proceed to the next scenario or return to the main menu.

docs.title: Scenario 2 User Guide: Driving to a Job Interview
Overview
docs.text:
In Scenario 2, you play as Mike as he drives to a civilian job interview. This experience is designed to highlight some of the coordination challenges he encounters during everyday driving tasks.

Throughout the scenario, you will follow guided on-screen objectives, interact with vehicle controls, and hear Mike’s internal thoughts at key points during the drive.

docs.title: Important Platform Note
docs.text:
Scenario 2 is currently recommended for use in the desktop application version.

At this time, the APK version is not recommended for this scenario due to stability and performance issues. In the APK build, the level may take a long time to load and may crash shortly after gameplay begins, often within the first few interactions.

For the most reliable experience, please play Scenario 2 using the desktop application version.

docs.title: Player Positioning and Play Style
docs.text:
At the start of the level, during the controls tutorial, you should be positioned just outside the driver-side door and within comfortable reach of the door handle. You will remain in this position until you complete Objective 1: Open the driver-side door.

After completing Objective 1, you are automatically moved into the vehicle. Once repositioned, you should be seated in the driver's seat and see the scene from Mike’s perspective at approximately eye level, including visibility of his torso and legs.

For the best experience, it is recommended that you choose either seated play or standing play for the entire scenario and remain consistent throughout. Switching between standing and sitting during the scenario can affect player placement and make interactions more difficult.

docs.title: Movement in Scenario 2
docs.text:
Unlike some other scenarios in the project, Scenario 2 does not use teleport movement.

You do not need to move yourself around the environment during this level. Instead, you are automatically positioned where you need to be at each stage of the scenario. Your focus should remain on completing the current objective and interacting with the vehicle controls using the required hand inputs.

docs.title: Reorienting Your View
docs.text:
Scenario 2 is intended to be played through the desktop application using Meta Quest Link. If you are not positioned correctly at the start of the level or after entering the vehicle, you can re-center your view through Meta Quest Link:

  • Press the Meta button on the right controller
  • Select Reset View

This should help place you back in the correct position for the scenario.

docs.title: In-Game Menu Note
docs.text:
The Menu button with the three horizontal lines on the left controller opens an in-game settings menu. The same menu can also be opened using the left-hand watch widget. However, those settings are not currently functional in Scenario 2.

At this time, those menu options should not be relied on while playing this level.

docs.title: Controls and Settings
docs.text: Before the scenario begins, a tutorial screen explains the controls used throughout the experience and lets you choose optional voice and subtitle settings.

docs.title: Controller Interactions
docs.text:
The scenario uses the following controller inputs:

  • Right Trigger — Press buttons and confirm interactions, such as starting the car or accepting the phone call.
  • Right Grip — Grab and pull controls toward or away from yourself, such as the seatbelt, gear shift, and windshield wipers.
  • Left Grip — Grab and rotate or toggle controls using wrist motion, such as the turn signal lever and light controls.

docs.title: Voice and Subtitle Options
docs.text:
The tutorial screen also includes two settings:

  • Enable Voice (AI Speech) is enabled by default. When this setting is left on, you will hear Mike’s spoken dialogue during the scenario.
  • Enable Subtitles can be turned on before starting the scenario. When enabled, subtitles will appear during dialogue.

You may use voice only, subtitles only, both together, or disable voice for a quieter experience.

docs.title: Important Interaction Note
docs.text:
The tutorial screen includes a note that the turn signal and windshield wiper controls require a larger-than-expected pull to activate. If one of these interactions does not trigger right away, continue the motion farther until the objective registers.

After reviewing the controls and settings, select Continue to begin the scenario.

docs.title: Objective HUD
docs.text:
As you progress through the scenario, an Objective HUD appears on screen to guide you through each step.

The Objective HUD shows:

  • your current objective number out of 23,
  • the task you need to complete,
  • an interaction hint explaining how to perform the action,
  • and a progress bar showing how far you are in the scenario.

Use this HUD as your primary guide throughout the drive.

docs.title: Scenario Flow
docs.text:
Scenario 2 is broken into 23 guided objectives. These objectives walk you through the full driving experience from entering the car to arriving at the destination and exiting the vehicle.

The table below provides a user-facing summary of the scenario steps.

Objective Task 1 Open the driver-side door 2 Fasten your seatbelt 3 Start the car 4 Shift to reverse 5 Shift to drive 6 Turn on the left turn signal 7 Turn off the high beams and turn on the low beams 8 Turn on the windshield wipers 9 Turn on your right turn signal 10 Turn off your right turn signal 11 Accept the incoming call 12 Turn on your right turn signal 13 Turn off your right turn signal 14 Turn on your right turn signal 15 Turn off your right turn signal 16 Turn on your right turn signal 17 Shift to park 18 Turn off your right turn signal 19 Turn off the windshield wipers 20 Turn off the low beams 21 Turn off the car 22 Unfasten your seatbelt 23 Open the driver-side door

docs.title: What to Expect During the Scenario
docs.text: At a high level, Scenario 2 follows this progression:

docs.title: Entering and Preparing the Vehicle
docs.text: You begin outside the vehicle and complete the initial setup steps needed before driving, including opening the door, fastening your seatbelt, starting the car, and shifting gears.

docs.title: Driving Tasks
docs.text: During the drive, you will complete guided actions involving the vehicle’s lights, windshield wipers, turn signal, and an incoming phone call. Follow each objective carefully and use the on-screen interaction hint when needed.

docs.title: Parking and Shutdown
docs.text: At the end of the route, you will park the vehicle, turn off active controls, shut off the car, unfasten your seatbelt, and open the door to exit.

docs.title: Dialogue and Accessibility
docs.text:
Scenario 2 includes Mike’s internal thoughts as part of the experience.

Depending on the settings you choose at the start:

  • you may hear AI-generated speech,
  • you may see subtitles,
  • or you may use both together.

Because Enable Voice (AI Speech) is turned on by default, spoken dialogue will play unless you manually disable it before beginning the scenario.

docs.title: Scenario Completion
docs.text:
After all objectives are completed, the scenario ends on a completion screen.

From this screen, you can:

  • return to the main menu,
  • load the Morning Routine scenario,
  • or load the Grocery Run and Preparing Dinner scenario.

docs.title: Tips for Best Experience
docs.text:
• Follow the Objective HUD and wait for the next objective to appear before performing the next interaction.
  • Even if you already know what comes next, do not complete actions early. Performing an interaction before it is shown on the current objective may prevent the scenario from progressing correctly.
  • If the turn signal or windshield wiper interaction does not trigger immediately, continue the motion farther until it registers.
  • Voice is enabled by default, so disable it before starting if you do not want spoken dialogue.
  • Turn on subtitles before starting if you want text support during dialogue.

docs.title: AI Speech Issues
docs.text:
In some playthroughs, Mike’s AI voice may not behave exactly as intended.

  • In rare cases, the AI may say the word “function” instead of the expected dialogue.
  • In some sections, especially during the phone conversation, spoken dialogue may be cut off or may not exactly match the subtitle text.

These issues do not prevent you from completing the scenario. If the AI speaks incorrectly at the start of the level, exiting and restarting the scenario may resolve the issue.

docs.title: Objective Progression
docs.text:
Always wait for the Objective HUD to display your next task before performing it.

Even if you are replaying the scenario and already know the sequence, completing objectives early can interfere with scenario progression and may prevent later steps from registering correctly. For the best experience, complete each interaction only when it is shown as the current objective.

docs.title: Saving and Progress Tracking
docs.text:
Scenario 2 is designed to be completed in a single session and does not support resuming from a specific objective partway through the level.

In addition, scenario completion or progress may not always appear correctly in progress-tracking interfaces after finishing the experience. This means that even if you complete the scenario successfully, the completion percentage may not always update as expected on other menu or progress screens.
